title: Count Equal Parts in a Whole
description: Determine the total number of equal parts a whole is partitioned into.
condition: A shape or strip is partitioned into equal-sized parts.
response: Count the total number of equal parts to determine the denominator.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts, the denominator is $6$.
notes: Supports identifying thirds, fourths, sixths, etc.

title: Define a Unit Fraction
description: Recognize that a unit fraction represents one equal part of a whole partitioned into equal parts.
condition: A fraction of the form $\frac{1}{n}$ is given.
response: Interpret it as 1 out of $n$ equal parts of a whole.
label: Concept

title: Identify Equal Partitions
description: Determine whether a shape is partitioned into equal-sized parts.
condition: A shape is divided into multiple regions.
response: Judge whether all parts are equal in size.
label: Concept
example: A circle divided into 3 congruent sectors is partitioned into equal parts.
notes: Key for distinguishing correct from distractor choices.

title: Match Visual Model to Unit Fraction
description: Match a partitioned and shaded model to the corresponding unit fraction.
condition: A shape or strip shows 1 shaded part out of $n$ equal parts.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 1 of 8 equal parts is shaded, the fraction is $\frac{1}{8}$.
notes: Integrates counting equal parts and unit fraction meaning.

title: Partition a Shape into Equal Parts
description: Divide a given whole into a specified number of equal parts.
condition: Instruction to partition a whole into $n$ equal parts.
response: Draw or imagine $n$ equal-sized regions within the whole.
label: Production / Skill
example: Partition a rectangle into 6 equal parts by drawing 5 evenly spaced lines to create 6 equal sections.

title: Shade Specified Number of Equal Parts
description: Shade the number of equal parts indicated by the numerator.
condition: A fraction $\frac{a}{n}$ is given and the whole is partitioned into $n$ equal parts.
label: Principle / Model
example: For $\frac{1}{6}$, shade 1 of the 6 equal parts.

title: Sort Shapes by Number of Equal Parts
description: Classify shapes based on how many equal parts they are partitioned into.
condition: Multiple shapes are shown with different partitions.
response: Group each shape according to whether it is partitioned into thirds, fourths, sixths, or not equal parts.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If a circle is divided into 6 equal sectors, place it under 'Partitioned into Sixths.'
notes: Requires counting and checking equality of parts.
